meta.title: Projects — Angus Duncan
meta.heading: Projects
meta.subheading: All 166 of them — every repo, tool, site, bot, and experiment. Some are live products, some died in a day. That's the point.

projects.title: AI Cold-Calling Research
projects.tagline: How fast does an AI have to talk before the other end stops hanging up? A research brief.

projects.title: Carly SEO
projects.tagline: SEO console for a wedding photographer whose name must never appear in the rankings it's winning.

projects.title: Cold 1M Machine
projects.tagline: Planned a million cold emails, fact-checked itself, and concluded: don't send a million cold emails.

projects.title: Cold Call Bot
projects.tagline: A Claude agent hunts 5-star businesses with no website and turns them into a Google Doc call list.

projects.title: Cold Email Deliverability Playbook
projects.tagline: Everything learned sending cold at scale, written down so the next domain doesn't die finding out.

projects.title: Inbox Engine
projects.tagline: One domain sending 5,000 cold emails/day gets burned. A hundred domains sending 50 each don't.

projects.title: Inbox Radar
projects.tagline: Listens for people drowning in their inbox, drafts a helpful reply, and waits for my approval.

projects.title: Lead Agent
projects.tagline: A whiteboard tree of agents that finds leads, emails them, and books the Zoom call before you wake up.

projects.title: LeadKit
projects.tagline: The $100/mo outreach SaaS, except it's ~240 Node agents on your own machine and mostly runs on $0.

projects.title: Leads Cold Call
projects.tagline: Scrape realtors off Google Maps for free, then a call cockpit A/B tests which price closes.

projects.title: Outreach Engine
projects.tagline: The 24/7 cold-email pipeline, generalized: 13 module docs, a control panel, and a 149-task roadmap.

projects.title: SendLab
projects.tagline: Your own Resend, in a box: routes every email to the cheapest provider, with failover and a Mac app.

projects.title: Website Builder
projects.tagline: No website? We already built you one. That'll be $50.

projects.title: Website Scaler
projects.tagline: Ten agents find businesses with no website, build them one, and email a $50 invoice-shaped offer.

projects.title: X Growth
projects.tagline: Drafts replies and reposts in my voice on the free AI stack. Posts nothing until I say so.

projects.title: Creator Hunt
projects.tagline: Finds 10k-follower founders on four platforms and offers them $50 to post. Refuses to send without a mailing address.

projects.title: Heynox Tunnel
projects.tagline: Fly a WebGL tunnel of notifications to reach the Nox vault. Technically, an ad

projects.title: heynox-ad
projects.tagline: A 9-second Revolut-style TikTok ad for RPLY, rendered for $0 — down to the synthesized soundtrack.

projects.title: heynox-higgsfield
projects.tagline: The tunnel ad again, but AI-video: WebGL frames in, Higgsfield cinematics out.

projects.title: heynox-proto
projects.tagline: "Everyone is screaming at you. One calm inbox." — landing-page prototypes for the NOX pitch.

projects.title: heynox-referral
projects.tagline: A one-file NOX fan landing page with a slot for your referral code. Swap one constant, deploy anywhere.

projects.title: HopperLeads: heynox edition
projects.tagline: The homeowner-leads desktop app, rebadged to drip 116 scored RPLY influencer prospects.

projects.title: howdynox
projects.tagline: A static mirror of the RPLY site with exactly one job: hijack every download button.

projects.title: howdynox-app
projects.tagline: The real heynox.com source, redeployed on a second domain so the campaign never touches prod.

projects.title: NOX Bullet
projects.tagline: A bullet fires into the logo. Click to fire again. That's it, that's the site

projects.title: nox-domain-sites
projects.tagline: 22 domains, 22 AI-written brand voices, one script to attach them all to Vercel.

projects.title: nox-explode
projects.tagline: Click anywhere, detonate an explosion in slow motion. There's a slider for how slow.

projects.title: nox-logo-explode
projects.tagline: Two more ways to destroy the NOX logo: detonate, or shatter it like glass.

projects.title: nox-redirect
projects.tagline: A tiny 302 with a memory — token-checked download links that log every click.

projects.title: RPLY Console
projects.tagline: The campaign war room: influencer finder, burn-and-replace mail pool, and a go-live runbook.

projects.title: RPLY Contacts
projects.tagline: An iPhone app that cleans your address book — first names, monogram avatars, merged dupes. All on-device.

projects.title: RPLY Phone
projects.tagline: RPLY on iPhone via the only loophole iOS allows: a keyboard that reads your clipboard.

projects.title: rply-launch
projects.tagline: The Product Hunt + Show HN launch package for RPLY, written and waiting on founder sign-off.

projects.title: AI Brain
projects.tagline: Chat with a 3D brain that fires while it thinks, then hands you the prompting lesson. It remembers.

projects.title: AI Stack Map
projects.tagline: Click a task type and watch energy pulse along the wires to whichever brain actually handles it.

projects.title: animforge
projects.tagline: Tells a free model to improve the animation, screenshots it, and argues until it renders clean.

projects.title: brainstorm
projects.tagline: Type one sentence, get a full project plan filed away for the day you have time to build it.

projects.title: cheat-glasses
projects.tagline: Camera sees the homework, Claude solves it, the answer arrives in your ear. The name says what it's for.

projects.title: Faceless
projects.tagline: One daemon running ten anonymous TikTok brands: writes, voices, renders, schedules. Nobody on camera.

projects.title: Graham
projects.tagline: Feed it a dossier and it reads a person the way Graham Duncan would — rubric distilled from his actual essays.

projects.title: Jarvis
projects.tagline: Call the number and Claude picks up. Text it and it texts back. Memory, tools, and a phone line — no app.

projects.title: Jarvis Dashboard
projects.tagline: Seventeen tabs, a ticker tape, a pulsing brain-eye, and a $100k scoreboard. Iron Man suit not included.

projects.title: laptop-agent
projects.tagline: Queue a task, a headless Claude drives the Mac's GUI itself. Stuck on 2FA? It texts me and waits.

projects.title: lib
projects.tagline: One require() every script routes AI calls through — free providers first, paid API as a genuine last resort.

projects.title: local-llm
projects.tagline: OpenAI's open-weight 20B running on the laptop — ChatGPT-style UI included, wifi optional.

projects.title: Lockin
projects.tagline: Close the laptop mid-thought. Tomorrow every conversation and terminal is exactly where you left it.

projects.title: malware-scanner
projects.tagline: Six engines and a jury of free models vote on whether a website is hostile. 0% false positives, by design.

projects.title: Mindread
projects.tagline: Paste the message you're about to send. Find out how it lands before the other person tells you.

projects.title: oneword
projects.tagline: A chatbot that answers anything — essay prompts, panics, jailbreak attempts — in exactly one word. Yes.

projects.title: ruflo-test
projects.tagline: What a 15-agent Byzantine-consensus swarm framework leaves behind when you point it at an empty repo.

projects.title: samewave
projects.tagline: Log in with Spotify and it scores your vibe against someone else's out of 100, songs to bridge included.

projects.title: SavicAI
projects.tagline: Watches a TikTok editor's whole catalog, learns his transition mix, then cuts your footage like he would.

projects.title: Second Take
projects.tagline: Your command failed? A free AI already diagnosed it before Claude starts flailing. Same wall never costs twice.

projects.title: Sira
projects.tagline: Siri with one letter swapped and the brain replaced: Apple ears, Claude thoughts, spoken back out loud.

projects.title: spend-guard
projects.tagline: A watchdog with a kill-switch, so a runaway AI bot costs at most $20 before everything shuts off and texts me.

projects.title: viral-x
projects.tagline: Reverse-engineers why tweets go viral, writes its own, and learns from real view counts. 100% local, $0.

projects.title: voice-caller
projects.tagline: An AI that dials a lead, holds a real conversation, books the follow-up, and hangs up. FCC disclosure built in.

projects.title: brain-sync
projects.tagline: Turns the whole machine into one wikilinked Obsidian map — every tool, skill, agent, and repo a node.

projects.title: DeepWorkScore
projects.tagline: Scores your day 0–100 from what you worked on plus how well you prompted. Doom-scrolling costs points.

projects.title: dropkit
projects.tagline: AirDrop as a CLI: fleet fan-out, watch folders, live radar. Refuses to spam strangers on principle.

projects.title: EyeSite Saver
projects.tagline: Every 20 minutes it takes over your screen until you look 20 feet away for 20 seconds. Non-negotiable.

projects.title: file-warden
projects.tagline: A janitor for Home, Desktop, and Downloads. Idle files get filed by type and month. Never deletes.

projects.title: Ghostype
projects.tagline: Cursor's Tab-autocomplete, except in Messages, Mail, and everywhere else you type. Learns your voice.

projects.title: Habit Warden
projects.tagline: A habit tracker that blocks your distractions and plays David Goggins at you when you slack off.

projects.title: Lockless
projects.tagline: Locked yourself out of Screen Time? Brute-force your own forgotten PIN out of an old iTunes backup.

projects.title: MathSnap
projects.tagline: Drag-select any math problem on screen; Claude solves it and reads the answer aloud. Raycast-ready.

projects.title: morning-brief
projects.tagline: A daily machine status text with no cloud and no MCP — so it still fires when everything else is down.

projects.title: OpenClaw Warden
projects.tagline: A parole officer for jailbroken local models: timed sessions, RAM checks, and a kill switch.

projects.title: ReplyKey
projects.tagline: An iOS keyboard with one extra key: ✨. Tap it and it writes your reply, in any app, for $0.

projects.title: shrink
projects.tagline: Squeeze text before it hits an LLM. Heuristic for free, or --ai for a 2–5× semantic cut that keeps the facts.

projects.title: skillify
projects.tagline: Point it at a script or repo and it becomes a permanent Claude skill. The 'now do this forever' button.

projects.title: SpellFix
projects.tagline: iPhone autocorrect for your Mac. Fixes teh → the in every app, leaves your lol alone.

projects.title: stack-doctor
projects.tagline: One command, green/red lines on the whole AI stack. Catches silent breakage before it burns tokens.

projects.title: startup-claude
projects.tagline: Log in, watch a boot animation, and land in six terminals already running Claude — one per project.

projects.title: term-grid
projects.tagline: Twelve real terminals in a browser grid. Every cell is a live PTY, not a log — type into any of them.

projects.title: term-tile
projects.tagline: One command and every terminal window snaps into a neat grid. Stdlib only, zero dependencies.

projects.title: webshot
projects.tagline: Screenshot any URL or HTML file to PNG in one command. Won't hang on long-polling sites.

projects.title: Agent·SB
projects.tagline: I'll come to your house in Santa Barbara and install an AI agent on your computer. Pricing on request.

projects.title: Agentpath
projects.tagline: A one-page pitch for custom AI agents, where the demo agent does the selling

projects.title: AI VFX Reel
projects.tagline: Feed it a logo, an emoji, or your face — get back a viral-style 9:16 VFX reel for about $1.50.

projects.title: Alpha Bank
projects.tagline: A real-time bank for classroom play-money, replacing the spreadsheet the class broke

projects.title: Alpha Sites
projects.tagline: Finds businesses with no website, builds them one with Claude, emails a $50 offer

projects.title: Angus Agent Maker
projects.tagline: Drop your phone number, I text you, you get a Claude-powered agent for your business.

projects.title: angusangus.com
projects.tagline: My calling card: an ∞ glyph sphere-traced in 3D and printed entirely out of flowing type.

projects.title: Animations
projects.tagline: The Blender models, renders, and scripts feeding visuals into everything else.

projects.title: APEX
projects.tagline: GPS telemetry for kids on Surrons — record a run, get splits, claim the podium

projects.title: AWS Console Clone
projects.tagline: EC2, S3, Lambda and CloudWatch, faithfully cloned — minus the bill

projects.title: Claude Anim
projects.tagline: The Claude logo, animated in five acts, in your terminal, with zero dependencies.

projects.title: Crazy Web Effects
projects.tagline: Every expensive agency web effect, rebuilt as 31 skills you can just ask for

projects.title: Crew Kit
projects.tagline: The madebyangus tiny construction crew as React components: fifteen workers, a crane, an hourglass.

projects.title: Face FX
projects.tagline: Three MediaPipe models watch your webcam and turn you into WebGL effects. Your face never leaves the tab.

projects.title: Flowtype Site
projects.tagline: I reverse-engineered andrew.net's flowing-type 3D glyph and made it a generator. Two sites shipped from it.

projects.title: Flux Store
projects.tagline: A complete cart-to-checkout storefront for desk gadgets. Sells nothing, convincingly

projects.title: Fundraiser QR
projects.tagline: Prop an old phone on the table, whole room scans to donate. Turns any retired iPhone into a kiosk.

projects.title: Gesture VFX
projects.tagline: Neon trails, ghost echoes, and beat-synced clones tracked onto my own footage. Free, local, no AI video.

projects.title: Lusion Wallpaper
projects.tagline: Six octaves of noise pretending to be a lava lamp, following your cursor around

projects.title: Made by Angus
projects.tagline: The portfolio site, plus four complete fake agencies living in its basement

projects.title: Made by Angus v8
projects.tagline: Click the 3D laptop, fly into its screen — which is a real, clickable client site

projects.title: Made by Angus v9
projects.tagline: v8's sequel. The README still says v8 — the version number outran the docs

projects.title: madebyangus Models
projects.tagline: Low-poly laptops and monitors, with paperwork on which model goes where.

projects.title: Mini Men
projects.tagline: Tiny construction workers build a giant pizza. $2 a video, and someone always falls in the oven

projects.title: OnlyWebsites
projects.tagline: A self-running cold-email bot selling $1k websites to OnlyFans creators, at $0 in AI spend

projects.title: ORBITAL
projects.tagline: A CRT ground station that sweeps live satellite imagery for abandoned buildings. 64 real ruins pre-pinned

projects.title: PLINTH
projects.tagline: Serious furniture for unserious objects. The UFO lamp comes with a cow, non-negotiable

projects.title: Profile Site
projects.tagline: The cold-email fleet's fake personas get a gallery — a friend names them and uploads headshots, live

projects.title: Resend Clone
projects.tagline: Resend's landing page and dashboard, reproduced in plain HTML to learn why its UI is good

projects.title: Satellite SB
projects.tagline: ORBITAL's deprecated twin, kept as a monument to editing the wrong repo

projects.title: Site Forge
projects.tagline: Five hand-built design systems so a website generator never has to improvise CSS.

projects.title: Solei Sitter
projects.tagline: A babysitter's booking site where the form texts her phone directly. No dashboard, no database.

projects.title: Tiger
projects.tagline: A tiger gallops across the page forever, spelled out of the word "tiger". That's it. That's the site.

projects.title: TouchDesigner Kit
projects.tagline: Scripts that interrogate TouchDesigner until it admits where the hand-tracking data lives.

projects.title: Website Maker
projects.tagline: An agent that builds websites, grades its own work 0–100, and retries until it clears the bar

projects.title: Wiggle
projects.tagline: AI that writes the 'can I please…' text to your parents, argued well enough to land

projects.title: Biz Finder
projects.tagline: A local llama brainstorms business models; RDAP proves nobody owns the name. $0 in API bills.

projects.title: Depop Flipper
projects.tagline: Sell the jacket first, buy it after. A no-inventory Depop arbitrage desk in one index.html.

projects.title: Depop Scout
projects.tagline: Drives my own Chrome through Depop and flags anything priced way under its cohort's median.

projects.title: Domain Grabber
projects.tagline: AI brainstorms brandable domains; the registry itself says if they're free. No API keys.

projects.title: Domain Master
projects.tagline: Brute-forces the entire 3-character .com space against Verisign RDAP, hunting for drops.

projects.title: Handlehunt
projects.tagline: Is @thatname free on GitHub, TikTok, Twitch and seven others? One command, no logins.

projects.title: Larp Shopify
projects.tagline: Maple & Stone Co. — a complete storefront for a store that does not exist. That's the point.

projects.title: Profile Namer
projects.tagline: Infers people's real names from how they text you. Yes, it reads your iMessage history.

projects.title: ShopfiyCompleter
projects.tagline: A full multi-store Shopify admin panel. Yes, the name is misspelled. No, I'm not fixing it.

projects.title: Shopify Sim
projects.tagline: A fake Shopify dashboard that pushes fake sale notifications to your very real phone.

projects.title: Snack Shack
projects.tagline: A point-of-sale for an actual snack shack, running on a Google Sheet. Hosting cost: zero.

projects.title: Underground 805 Theme
projects.tagline: The brand's front door: noise, scanlines, a blinking LIVE dot, EST. 805 / SANTA BARBARA.

projects.title: Underground Brand Sheets
projects.tagline: SMS list building at $0/month: storefront phone capture straight into a Google Sheet I own.

projects.title: UndergroundBrandd
projects.tagline: The whole streetwear-brand operating manual: theme, ads, SMS, suppliers — ~60 playbooks deep.

projects.title: Viral Kit
projects.tagline: The playbook for taking 50 private repos public without buying stars or doxxing myself.

projects.title: Web Projects
projects.tagline: The junk drawer: client sites, two Shopify themes, and a Screen Time remover in one monorepo.

projects.title: Engram
projects.tagline: A free Enneagram test with wings, tritype, and a tiebreaker built to get past your ego's answers.

projects.title: Habit Tracker
projects.tagline: Habit reminders that text you via iMessage — but only if you haven't already done the thing.

projects.title: hour-tracker
projects.tagline: A time tracker styled as a rocket launch console, because a plain timer wasn't going to get used.

projects.title: How Tech Works
projects.tagline: One page explaining how technology actually works. Writing it down is how you learn it.

projects.title: Kairos GP
projects.tagline: The classroom tracker as a Grand Prix: students are race cars, good work moves them up the track.

projects.title: Kairos Tracker
projects.tagline: Every student is a rocket. A gamified classroom tracker in one 12,000-line HTML file.

projects.title: Khan Academy Video for Math Academy
projects.tagline: Math Academy tells you what to learn, not how. This puts the right Khan video one click away.

projects.title: Khan Booster
projects.tagline: The Khan-video extension, round two: saved mappings, history, and a no-API-key fallback.

projects.title: learn-smth
projects.tagline: Type "Kafka", get a sim game that teaches it — with a solvability proof so every level can be won.

projects.title: Math Academy Agent
projects.tagline: Claude does the math homework, typing answers at a suspiciously human pace.

projects.title: Math Academy Solver
projects.tagline: The v2: now it can see the diagrams. Auto-solves whole tasks until it gets one wrong.

projects.title: Prompting Course
projects.tagline: A five-module course on prompting Claude, from "what is an LLM" to agents. One HTML file.

projects.title: rust-study
projects.tagline: Learning Rust with rustlings, clippy, and a $0 AI tutor. The borrow checker is the curriculum.

projects.title: skill-learner
projects.tagline: Reads my Claude Code transcripts for corrections and writes them into memory. Fool me once.

projects.title: Coin Sniper
projects.tagline: pump.fun mints 1,000 coins an hour. This texts me the handful where the creator put real SOL in.

projects.title: Kalshi Copybot
projects.tagline: Tried to copy Kalshi's best traders. The tape is anonymous, so it stalks large prints instead.

projects.title: PitchBet
projects.tagline: Play-money prediction market for pitch competitions — the audience bets fake cash on who wins, odds move live.

projects.title: Polymarket Copybot
projects.tagline: Follows Polymarket whales with a $17 bankroll and five layers of risk checks between it and a trade.

projects.title: Revenue Dashboard
projects.tagline: One page answering the only question that matters: is anything actually making money?

projects.title: Camera Watch
projects.tagline: Maps the license-plate-reader cameras near you, so you know exactly when you're being recorded.

projects.title: Caspian
projects.tagline: A game that so far is just a machine for generating 2,000 fake in-game emails trying to sell you something.

projects.title: Dad's Birthday Sites
projects.tagline: Every year my dad gets a birthday website. This is three years of the tradition.

projects.title: Ghostbike
projects.tagline: A stealth range computer that tells your Sur-Ron how far it can really go, not the optimistic number on the dash.

projects.title: Hacking Resources Mirror
projects.tagline: 14,600 infosec cheatsheets and payloads, harvested text-only so you can read them without running anything.

projects.title: Macintosh Disk
projects.tagline: A Mac OS 8 boot disk chopped into 95MB chunks so a browser can Range-fetch and boot it.

projects.title: Meme Race
projects.tagline: Turn $20 into $10K before Will does — leveraged meme-coin bets racing a rival down a synthwave 3D track.

projects.title: RobloxBR
projects.tagline: A Rivals-style Roblox battle royale, written as readable Rojo source instead of a black-box place file.

projects.title: Sneaking Out
projects.tagline: A location spoofer with an honest name — push fake GPS to a tethered iPhone and walk a route you're not on.

projects.title: 805 Agents
projects.tagline: AI agents built and installed in person for Santa Barbara businesses. Flat $500, then monthly.

projects.title: CF Migrate
projects.tagline: One idempotent script to repoint 22 Porkbun domains at Cloudflare DNS. Dry-run first.

projects.title: Glitchpilled
projects.tagline: A PIL script that prints one persona's banner and pfp in an NZT gold-and-cyan glitch palette.

projects.title: Insta Bots
projects.tagline: An Instagram-bot idea that never got past its README. An empty repo, told honestly.

projects.title: Night Shift
projects.tagline: Boot the Mac, watch a sci-fi startup sequence, and land in six terminals already running Claude.

projects.title: PycharmProjects
projects.tagline: The default PyCharm hello-world, committed for an honest catalog. It does nothing.

projects.title: Term Organizer
projects.tagline: Inventory, tile, and cull twenty open Terminal windows without touching the mouse.

projects.title: UG Social
projects.tagline: Point it at a Shopify store and it renders daily 9:16 product videos with a local model — $0, no editor.

projects.title: Work Report
projects.tagline: Turns a week of git commits into a styled report someone would actually read.
